recentTweets.text: Two Ukrainian Pilots Lobbied for F-16s. They Won’t Get to See If the Jets Help Fend Off Russia.  Our story about Juice & Moonfish w/ @laraseligman + @JaneLytv https://t.co/7hv1eaMJGF

Zhenya’s escorting work hasn’t taken as much of a hit from #Russia’s invasion as other sectors of #Ukraine’s economy have. If anything, the influx of journalists and NGOs has provided a new client base for him https://t.co/2ibZUjL0Dw

“It still feels unreal sometimes,” said @evangershkovich's sister Danielle, reflecting on the 112 days since he was wrongfully detained while on a reporting assignment in #Russia @WSJ https://t.co/3zUn7Fnsaj
Three long weeks since our friend & colleague @evangershkovich was detained in #Russia while on a reporting assignment. Help us keep him at the top of the agenda https://t.co/5sVyzcG9NI

It’s been two weeks now since our friend and colleague, Evan Gershkovich, was wrongfully detained by Russia for doing his job. We stand with Evan and his family in demanding his immediate release #IStandWithEvan @WSJ https://t.co/r0jq3bsgzv

Inside #Russia's notorious Lefortovo prison where our colleague @evangershkovich has been held for a week “You cannot see anybody, and you are completely alone... There is not a noise, nothing" https://t.co/bXJzhWQ16Q #IStandWithEvan #FreeEvan

It's been five days since our colleague & friend @evangershkovich was taken into custody by officers with the Russian Federal Security Service while on assignment outside Moscow. Little is known about his condition or well-being https://t.co/v0YKRMloAG #IStandWithEvan #FreeEvan

One of the things that always struck me about @evangershkovich is how easy he makes reporting look. As friend & fellow journalist @polinaivanovva says, for him it is "incredibly natural because he is an amazing talker + charms everybody + is very funny" https://t.co/d5PWBh6d4S

Chatting w/ @evangershkovich last week, I asked if he was enjoying life in Moscow. He said "enjoying" wasn't the right word, but that he was feeling more comfortable & there was a good crew of other journalists. Now he's in a #Russia-n jail. Join @WSJ in demanding his release https://t.co/4TD8JMSUvO
In just one year working for @WSJ our colleague @evangershkovich has done such great work. Read it here & join us in calling on #Russia to release him https://t.co/xzTjgMeRSU #IStandwithEvan

A glimpse inside Mariupol under #Russia-n occupation
https://t.co/bFNRsvZL3s

Russian soldiers forced the 3 brothers to their knees on the edge of a freshly dug pit in northern #Ukraine. The first bullet killed the youngest brother. The second hit the eldest. The round aimed for Mykola struck him close to his right ear. He survived. https://t.co/bhOnqZByGl

Echoes of #UkraineRussiaWar in #Finland: During the Battle for Raate Road in 1939, Finns cut Soviet supply lines. As the Soviets, who were mostly Ukrainians, struggled with the cold and harsh terrain, the Finns wore them down https://t.co/t5N0xuvQFn @SuneEngel

Before the war, Tymofiy collected coins. Now, in the wake of #Russia’s occupation, he also collects fragments of shrapnel ‘to have material evidence that this whole thing really happened’ #Ukraine  https://t.co/ADr0LFeiPY

Russians didn’t change their clocks to Ukrainian time when they invaded, so civilians and troops were running on two different clocks. “It was the difference between curfew times, that was when most people died” https://t.co/kKeOi0Md5b @tggrove

After @CathyOtten's investigation into problems at Balad airbase - in which military officials said only 5 F-16s were operational - pilots were made to fly 19 jets in 1 day as a show of capability. Ex-Iraq Army official described the sorties as "bullshit"
https://t.co/tvcxYRFfTw
A U.S. passport-holder in the time of #coronavirus can travel to almost as few countries as an #Iraq-i passport-holder can visit (without a visa) at the best of times

In com­ments to ⁦@alhurra⁩ a week be­fore his assassination ⁦@hushamalhashimi⁩ doubted #Iraq PM ⁦@MAKadhimi⁩ had the polit­i­cal will to take on #Iran-backed militias. The response to his killing may now serve as a test of that  https://t.co/lum4N4nzkX

Very honoured to have won @opcofamerica award with @renaeffendi for our stories about a Swedish man’s quest to recover his 7 orphaned grandhildren from the ruins of Islamic State’s caliphate in #Syria @WSJ

“We came here to­day to show our loy­alty to the na­tion—not to an indi­vid­ual,” said a med­ical stu­dent who de­clined to be iden­ti­fied for fear of get­ting in trou­ble with her par­ents if they found out she had come to the protests #Iraq https://t.co/cvNGIrlWjc

The Trump administration is preparing possible cuts of $250 million in military aid to #Iraq, funds already approved by Congress, in the event that the government expels U.S. troops, by ⁦@vmsalama⁩  https://t.co/Kud2kdeLuA
U.S. officials warned #Iraq that it risks losing access to its account at the New York Fed, where international oil sale revenue is kept, if it forces U.S. troops to leave  https://t.co/3nkD12VmOt
#Iraq-i parliament’s martyrs committee proposes changing the name of #Baghdad International Airport to “Abu Mahdi al-Mohandes International Airport” https://t.co/yN2F19qu7P

Such is the desperation in al-Hol that some mothers have signed away parental rights to facilitate the repatriation of their children. Rights groups are concerned governments are forcing mothers to give up their kids, contravening intl humanitarian law https://t.co/vcrleGpHXh

If the state’s forceful response to antigovernment protests shocked many #Iraq-is, it was grimly fa­mil­iar to the coun­try’s Sunni minor­ity, which for years has com­plained of ar­bi­trary de­ten­tions & abuses by se­cu­rity forces https://t.co/4GB6udZkox

Seven months ago, Patricio Galvez rescued his 7 grandchildren from the rubble of Islamic State’s caliphate in #Syria. But back home in #Sweden, he faced a new struggle with authorities over how to rehabilitate & reintegrate them  https://t.co/i949t0EWCW
Iraq’s prime minister says he will submit his resignation following weeks of violent antigovernment protests  https://t.co/fEWIxQgvF2
@Mustafa_salimb So did I!

#Iraq government unveils results of probe into violent crackdown on demonstrations that left more than 100 dead. Protesters preparing for fresh demonstrations on Friday unimpressed by findings, which don’t identify those responsible for shooting them  https://t.co/IFY8Gk14tq
“#Iraq’s PM removed blast walls from the streets of Baghdad & placed them on the Internet instead. He moved the physical barriers on our streets into our minds”
Pompeo confirmed attacks on Saudi oil infrastructure did not originate from #Iraq, according to (Iraqi) readout of call w/ PM @AdilAbdAlMahdi

“I know I have named my restaurant 'Hitler', but that does not mean that I love him,” Rebar Mohammed told ⁦@RudawEnglish⁩. “I have done it just to make my restaurant famous among people.” 🤦🏻‍♀️ https://t.co/jZbKonbFHZ
"And you had the Nobel Prize? That's incredible. They gave it to you for what reason?" ⁦@realDonaldTrump⁩ said to Yazidi advocate ⁦@NadiaMuradBasee⁩  https://t.co/7AUHlXZBER

Can #Iraq be a mediator in the Iran-US dispute? Baghdad needs to unify its foreign policy & fix its domestic politics before it can realistically mediate between two powers like Washington & Tehran. A reality check from ⁦@renadmansour⁩  https://t.co/RQDShOLrSd

“Even some corporate lawyers in #Iraq advise clients to use tribal councils rather than courts. This has led to a booming new business: sheikhs who rent out their services” https://t.co/JukFaBrvTk by @SimonaFoltyn @TheEconomist

Some additional details of how Baghdadi rolls from an interview last year with a senior Islamic State member who attended a meeting with him in spring/ summer 2017. Where is he now the group controls no territory? In short, we don’t really know  https://t.co/tNKfuFReaP
Companies hired by the governor of #Mosul on lucrative contracts to clear the debris of war work deliberately slowly, or sometimes do not exist https://t.co/RnxHgJYrnH by @JohnDrtrs
Forced underground, Islamic State is enlisting the help of women to sustain its insurgency in #Iraq, relying on them to distribute funds & purchase supplies https://t.co/JvfAG8tvWj w/ @alnabhan_ali

Fallujah refugees give babies, businesses Kurdish names after going home https://t.co/KEY8Nb2vWw A story from 2014 about displaced Arabs bringing a touch of Anbar to #Kurdistan https://t.co/l8ZdrQ814W #شقلوجة

The evolution of Qais al-Khazali — from outlaw to aspiring statesman — illustrates #Iraq’s changing political land landscape & poses a dilemma for the U.S. as it seeks to curb #Iran’s influence in the region https://t.co/rnrMiSir9C

The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad warned its citizens on Monday to remain indoors & shelter from falling projectiles. The threat was not from Islamic State but celebratory gunfire and fireworks it anticipated to mark one year after victory over the militants https://t.co/wFcjpzFnZn

Even though her Instagram & Snapchat videos were followed by millions of #Iraqi-s, very few people knew the real Tara Fares. @cathyotten paints a complex portrait of the social media star who was murdered earlier this year https://t.co/phvVh0CPyi
#Iraq-is design new banknote after central bank governor admits 7 billion dinars were destroyed when a vault flooded several years ago https://t.co/YMemabkGkd

Billboard in #Erbil hails the battles of Perde & Sheila last October in which peshmerga fighters clashed with #Iraq-i security forces following #Kurdistan independence referendum https://t.co/oUNI5D44gU

#Iraq, Myanmar and the Republic of Congo rank among the fastest-growing holiday destinations in the world, according to new research 
https://t.co/eyNH41YPjr

Of all the painful moments I’ve covered in #Iraq, perhaps the most harrowing was accompanying #Yazidi survivor @NadiaMuradBasee on her first return to Kocho, the village where she had been captured by Islamic State in 2014 https://t.co/uURwzyd9gq #NobelPeace #Iraq
In a show of solidarity with the people of Basra, EU ambassador falls ill after drinking contaminated water there @EUAmbIraq
Any ef­forts by @AdilAbdAlMahdi to make sig­nif­i­cant changes in #Iraq could be thwarted by vested in­ter­ests of the very par­ties that co­a­lesced be­hind his nom­i­na­tion https://t.co/MUMXWPmdTG

"Basra’s protests have been another step away from sectarian politics, toward a new political language & mobilization in which socioeconomic demands & regional identities become more salient” https://t.co/EaRDbNOJ1L by @harith_hasan #Iraq
Our story about the wrenching choices faced by Yazidi women who survived Islamic State’s depredations on today’s front page w/ @alnabhan_ali https://t.co/xqaSkDoCUw
Electricity shortages ignited protests across southern #Iraq earlier this month & begged the question: why can’t one of the world’s top oil producers keep the power on?  https://t.co/W077JaLrL6

“Recent wave of protests suggests that the next fault line in #Iraq will not be between Shiites, Sunnis, and Kurds, but between the people and the ruling class, which has failed to govern for the past 15 years,” writes @renadmansour https://t.co/NccHOrOetl

“If things don’t change, a day will come when the sleeping people will revolt like a volcano,” said a man @alnabhan_ali & I interviewed in Basra in March. Four months later... #Iraq https://t.co/reN5nfLb4b
Booze shops on #Baghdad’s Abu Nawas doing a roaring trade for #Eid #عيد_مبارك
https://t.co/cH0WFnmn6t
Iraq moves towards full manual recount of recent election results, naming a panel of judges to take over elections commission https://t.co/WnprXIJPVj @Reuters
What does Sadr City share with Islamabad & Riyadh? All three were devised by Greek architect Constantinos Apostolou Doxiadis. This might interest you @PatrickOsgood. Courtesy of @Wikipedia
The true mark of a firebrand: Muqtada al-Sadr uses emojis ❤️ https://t.co/CxdOGAVA0v

The utter devastation visited on Sunni areas occupied by Islamic State in #Iraq has extinguished meaningful resistance to the Shiite-led political order that took shape after ‘03, reducing sectarian tensions https://t.co/Eijkub2Hza cc @fanarhaddad

It is unprecedented for an #Iraq-i head of state to campaign in Erbil & shows Abadi’s efforts to secure votes beyond his own Shiite Arab constituency while burnishing image as leader for all. But it remains to be seen if he can make any inroad in Kurdistan https://t.co/onldfodmIB

Turkish ambassador to #Iraq visited site of consulate in Mosul & says #Turkey wants to rebuild it after it was ruined during ISIS occupation. He also toured site of Mosul airport & said Turkish companies could rehabilitate it https://t.co/fD4LbmkaNN
War against ISIS in Iraq caused $45.7 billion of damage to infrastructure, according to @WorldBank study https://t.co/ECYsUEsQeL
The female weightlifters of Sadr City #Iraq https://t.co/gjUBLPNnwh
#Mosul @McDonalds https://t.co/Nx4roiNUL5
Kurds putting up new defences on the road 40 km south of Erbil after #Iraq-i forces push into disputed territory https://t.co/ogGQ09H3t5
“We had a common enemy, but now things are changing” said Peshmerga commander on new front w/ Iraq forces post-ISIS https://t.co/oo7b0WqFRY
"Erdogan comments suggest Kurdish leadership may have underestimated Turkish anger over referendum" https://t.co/z8RcCcCClg @amberinzaman
“We have a language. We have a history. We have a geography. And we have suffered” - Kurd after voting in referendum https://t.co/pOrxoLiX6y
"It's true Qasim Soleimani was here and I met him," said @masoud_barzani in press conference on Kurdish independence referendum #Iraq
Leaflet (in Arabic) delivered to the door in Erbil (Ainkawa) lists 37 bad things that will happen if you vote "no" in Kurdish referendum https://t.co/1IHGlzUSOA
Q. Soleimani warned Kurds that #Iran would no longer restrain PMU from attacking peshmerga if referendum takes place https://t.co/O2jxcU7GRg
A show of Kurdish-Catalan solidarity. Each to hold a referendum on independence in coming weeks #Iraq #Spain https://t.co/56EdXox0h6
This extract from @cathyotten's book is a powerful antidote to the slew of lurid stories on Yazidi women under ISIS https://t.co/YYvhiRQ06e
"Iraq used to have 2 rivers: Tigris & Euphrates. Now we have a third: the river of blood," said Mosul morgue worker. https://t.co/eLuXj1EUAx
In the back pocket of a man executed in a wheat field in #Iraq was an ID. This is his story: https://t.co/mA4ZH5yFpO
One of the more unusual moments of my time in #Iraq: Abu Mehdi al-Muhandis asking if I am Yezidi #Sinjar
Owner of 1st booze shop to re-open in Mosul tries to keep a low profile, but hard not to notice stream of customers https://t.co/1JIhUhq9t0
Man who worked in Mosul morgue under IS says forced to fake cause of death on #Iraq-i militants' death certificates https://t.co/xST4p371wz
An Islamic State map of #Iraq's Nineveh province in a house used by militants in Mosul, Hayy al-Andalus https://t.co/vXzlYTrthT
Love the details of Arab YPG recruits learning about Ocalan from Kurdish instructors in this story by @LizSly https://t.co/oNKGn7gCQV
Trails left by coalition planes circling over Mosul as #Iraq-i forces begin second phase of operation against Islamic State https://t.co/aaqdA7zZG3
My favourite photo from the #Mosul campaign so far by @odd_andersen for @AFP via @mojobaghdad https://t.co/vcVNQIARf5
Good map showing wedge of territory taken by peshmerga in latest offensive east of Mosul #Iraq via @UniteKurdistan https://t.co/DuavS7C6Xc
Cartoon depicting exodus from #Kurdistan whilst politicians wrangle over presidency http://t.co/f4cK2RXseN
Storm clouds gathering over Erbil #Kurdistan -- another atmospheric photo of this dazzling metropolis by @amirani87 http://t.co/zNzN6ngugO
Erbil in all its rainwashed glory. Photo by @amirani87 #Kurdistan http://t.co/p5VEJi5H2g
Witnesses said soldiers stood by helplessly, some crying, as Shi'ite militiamen executed 72 civilians in Diyala #Iraq http://t.co/ht25VzWA7N
Map of this week's peshmerga offensive south of Mosul dam #Iraq http://t.co/hewTTUKkpn
New American, British and German recruits to YPG ranks #Syria #Kurds http://t.co/XXUiFPjtGj
Western YPG recruits (Jordan Matson second from left) via @Rebwarfexri #Syria #Turkey #Kurdistan http://t.co/X2qc2xUGaV
Price of 1 kilo tomatoes in Mosul quadrupled since Kurds retook Rabia border crossing w/ #Syria + cut IS supply route http://t.co/yq5kdakzvi
Women and children of Amerli celebrate as Shi'ite militia parade through town after IS siege broken #Iraq http://t.co/NamjOnyFLm

Shi'ite militia Asaib Ahl al-Haq entering Suleiman Beg with Kurdish peshmerga on Monday #Iraq http://t.co/fIeMHurH5t

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 74 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Isabel Coles. The social-media posture reads as security-first / counterterrorism frame because the strongest matched frame is security and counterterrorism language. Top matched terms: Iraq / Baghdad / Kirkuk, Kurds / Kurdish, Kurdistan, Erbil / Hewler.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around security and counterterrorism language. I count 37 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 14 security-frame hits, 6 supportive or diplomatic hits, 7 humanitarian/minority hits, and 6 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2020-12-10T10:33:45Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.

tweets.text: Two Ukrainian Pilots Lobbied for F-16s. They Won’t Get to See If the Jets Help Fend Off Russia.  Our story about Juice & Moonfish w/ @laraseligman + @JaneLytv https://t.co/7hv1eaMJGF

Zhenya’s escorting work hasn’t taken as much of a hit from #Russia’s invasion as other sectors of #Ukraine’s economy have. If anything, the influx of journalists and NGOs has provided a new client base for him https://t.co/2ibZUjL0Dw

“It still feels unreal sometimes,” said @evangershkovich's sister Danielle, reflecting on the 112 days since he was wrongfully detained while on a reporting assignment in #Russia @WSJ https://t.co/3zUn7Fnsaj
Three long weeks since our friend & colleague @evangershkovich was detained in #Russia while on a reporting assignment. Help us keep him at the top of the agenda https://t.co/5sVyzcG9NI

It’s been two weeks now since our friend and colleague, Evan Gershkovich, was wrongfully detained by Russia for doing his job. We stand with Evan and his family in demanding his immediate release #IStandWithEvan @WSJ https://t.co/r0jq3bsgzv

Inside #Russia's notorious Lefortovo prison where our colleague @evangershkovich has been held for a week “You cannot see anybody, and you are completely alone... There is not a noise, nothing" https://t.co/bXJzhWQ16Q #IStandWithEvan #FreeEvan

It's been five days since our colleague & friend @evangershkovich was taken into custody by officers with the Russian Federal Security Service while on assignment outside Moscow. Little is known about his condition or well-being https://t.co/v0YKRMloAG #IStandWithEvan #FreeEvan

One of the things that always struck me about @evangershkovich is how easy he makes reporting look. As friend & fellow journalist @polinaivanovva says, for him it is "incredibly natural because he is an amazing talker + charms everybody + is very funny" https://t.co/d5PWBh6d4S

Chatting w/ @evangershkovich last week, I asked if he was enjoying life in Moscow.
